root_text.sn17.12:1.3: idhāhaṁ, bhikkhave, ekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.sn17.12:1.4: ‘na cāyamāyasmā rūpiyapātiyāpi suvaṇṇacuṇṇaparipūrāya hetu sampajānamusā bhāseyyā’ti.
root_text.sn17.12:1.5: Tamenaṁ passāmi aparena samayena lābhasakkārasilokena abhibhūtaṁ pariyādiṇṇacittaṁ sampajānamusā bhāsantaṁ.

translation_text.sn17.12:1.3: When I’ve comprehended the mind of a certain individual, I understand:
translation_text.sn17.12:1.4: ‘This venerable would not tell a deliberate lie even for the sake of a silver pot filled with gold powder.’
translation_text.sn17.12:1.5: But some time later I see them tell a deliberate lie because their mind is overcome and overwhelmed by possessions, honor, and popularity.
translation_text.sn17.12:1.6: So grim are possessions, honor, and popularity. …”
